summarization.a: The figure displays 2θχ X-ray diffraction patterns of various lithium manganese oxide compounds at  different concentrations.
summarization.b: The figure shows θ−2θ X-ray diffraction patterns of lithium manganese oxide compounds

data_extraction.b:
| Intensity (arb. units) | °2θ of 10cLi-MnO₂ | °2θ of 50cLi-MnO₂ | °2θ of MnO₂ ref. |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lithiated phase | 17 | 17 | - |
| Li₁₋ₓMn₂O₄ (111) | 20 | 20 | - |
| Li₁₋ₓMn₂O₄ (400) | 44 | 44 | - |
| β-MnO₂ (100) | 21 | 21 | 21 |
| β-MnO₂ (200) | 42 | 42 | 42 |
| Extra peak (*) | 33 | - | 33 |

a.question: Does all the XRD diffractograms correspond to samples MnO2−Li−MnO2?
How many Lithium cycles were used?
a.answer:
Two samples consist of 
1. 10 cycles of Li 
2. 50 cycles of Li

b.question: What is the peak with highest intensity in the 2θ XRD measurements?
b.answer: The (131) peak shows the highest intensity in all the samples 2θ XRD measurements

b.question: How is Lithium intercalcation understood from the XRD diffractograms?
b.answer:
In the beginning of the lithium intercalation, three reflections of Li1−xMn2O4 are somehow visible, the reflection at an angle 44.4° being the strongest one. The reflection is indexed  as (400), and it decreases in intensity as the lithium intercalation proceeds. The other two reflections at 17.7° and 35.9° are lower in intensity in these samples. At around 32.6° in the out-of-plane θ−2θ measurements of 10cLi−MnO2, there
is an additional “impurity” reflection.
